title: A Study of JNJ-77242113 for the Treatment of Participants With Moderate to Severe Plaque Psoriasis (ICONIC-ADVANCE 2)

eligibility.text: Diagnosis of plaque psoriasis, with or without psoriatic arthritis (PsA), for at least 26 weeks prior to the first administration of study intervention
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a sufficient duration of disease presence to detect treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Total body surface area (BSA) greater than or equal to (>=)10 percent (%) at screening and baseline
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a minimum BSA threshold for study inclusion, impacting feasibility of data collection.

eligibility.text: Total psoriasis area and severity index (PASI) >=12 at screening and baseline
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a sufficient level of disease activity for treatment effect detection.

eligibility.text: Total investigator global assessment (IGA) >=3 at screening and baseline
eligibility.justification: This criterion assesses the severity of psoriasis, potentially indicating a higher risk of complications or treatment response.

eligibility.text: Candidate for phototherapy or systemic treatment for plaque psoriasis
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that participants are actively seeking treatment options, impacting feasibility of data collection and study recruitment.

eligibility.text: Nonplaque form of psoriasis (for example, erythrodermic, guttate, or pustular)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a specific type of psoriasis is included for treatment effect detection.

eligibility.text: Current drug-induced psoriasis (for example, a new onset of psoriasis or an exacerbation of psoriasis from beta blockers, calcium channel blockers, or lithium)
eligibility.justification: This criterion identifies potential triggers for psoriasis exacerbations, impacting safety and study design.

eligibility.text: A current diagnosis or signs or symptoms of severe, progressive, or uncontrolled renal, liver, cardiac, vascular, pulmonary, gastrointestinal, endocrine, neurologic, hematologic, rheumatologic, psychiatric, or metabolic disturbances
eligibility.justification: This criterion identifies potential underlying health conditions that could be exacerbated by the study intervention.

eligibility.text: Known allergies, hypersensitivity, or intolerance to JNJ-77242113, deucravacitinib or to any of the excipients or components of the study intervention
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient safety by excluding individuals with known adverse reactions to the study interventions.

eligibility.text: Major surgical procedure, (for example, requiring general anesthesia) within 8 weeks before screening, or will not have fully recovered from surgical procedure, or has a surgical procedure planned during the time the participant is expected to participate in the study
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that participants are physically capable of participating in the study and minimizes potential complications.

endpoints.text: Percentage of Participants Who Achieved an Investigator's Global Assessment (IGA) Score of 0 or 1 and Greater Than or Equal to (>=) 2-Grade Improvement From Baseline at Week 16
Percentage of Participants Who Achieved Psoriasis Area and Severity Index (PASI) 90 Response at Week 16
Percentage of Participants Who Achieved IGA Score of 0 at Week 16
Percentage of Participants Who Achieved PASI 75 Response at Weeks 4 and 16
Percentage of Participants Who Achieved PASI 90 Response at Week 8
Percentage of Participants Who Achieved PASI 100 Response at Week 16
Percentage of Participants Who Achieved Scalp Specific (ss)-IGA Score of 0 or 1 and >=2-Grade Improvement From Baseline at Week 16 Among Participants With a Baseline Ss-IGA Score >=2
Percentage of Participants Who Achieved Psoriasis Symptom and Signs Diary (PSSD) Symptom Score of 0 at Weeks 8 and 16 Among Participants With a Baseline PSSD Symptom Score >0
Percentage of Participants Who Achieved >=4-Point Improvement From Baseline in PSSD Itch Score at Weeks 4 and 16 Among Participants With a Baseline PSSD Itch Score >=4
Percentage of Participants Who Achieved an IGA Score of 0 or 1 and >=2-Grade Improvement From Baseline at Weeks 16 and 24
Percentage of Participants Who Achieved an IGA Score of 0 at Weeks 16 and 24

randomization.randomization_description: Stratified by Baseline, Pasi. Blocked randomization.

adaptive_designs.description: Standard fixed-design trial. No adaptive features detected.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_endpoints: Number of Participants With Treatment-emergent Adverse Events (TEAEs)
Number of Participants With Treatment-emergent Serious Adverse Events (TESAEs)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

pharmacogenetics.summary: Genes detected (TYK2) but could not map indication "plaque psoriasis" to a GWAS EFO trait. Try alternative trait name.
